response:
Sec. 1  
People who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their immune system reacts to peanut proteins in the same way it would react to any other allergen  
Living underwater does not eliminate exposure to peanut proteins which may be present in food or environmental sources  
The body's allergic response is triggered by the presence of allergens regardless of the environment in which a person lives  

Sec. 2  
Allergies are not affected by changes in environment such as living underwater  
The immune system continues to function in the same manner whether a person is on land or in water  
Therefore people with peanut allergies cannot live underwater because their condition is unrelated to their habitat
